pr:section.pr:title: LLM Descriptions
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Machine-readable descriptions embedded in schemas and layer files for language model consumption.
pr:section.pr:p: The LLM layer provides descriptions optimized for language models. It operates in two modes that serve different purposes:

pr:ul.pr:li: Out-of-band (llm.xml): a layer file containing two element types. node elements describe spec nodes by ID reference (validated via keyref in the combined schema). schema elements describe schemas or specific schema elements by namespace URI, with an optional element attribute to narrow the scope. This follows the standard layer-file pattern with independent evolution.

In-band (inside XSD files): llm:describe elements placed inside xs:appinfo annotations, co-located with the schema elements they describe. Any XSD can import the LLM namespace and add descriptions to its type definitions, element declarations, or top-level annotations.

pr:section.pr:p: The two modes complement each other. In-band descriptions travel with the schema and are available to any tool that reads the XSD. Out-of-band descriptions can reference nodes from any layer without modifying those layers, and can evolve independently of the schemas they describe.
The layer is self-describing: llm.xsd contains in-band descriptions of its own elements via xs:appinfo, and the clayers's llm.xml includes out-of-band descriptions of all schemas including itself.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding how LLM descriptions work in-band and out-of-band, and why the layer is self-describing.

rel:relation.rel:note: The LLM layer is one of the layers in the architecture, with a unique dual-mode design (in-band and out-of-band).

art:mapping.art:note: llm.xsd defines both the out-of-band elements (node, schema) and the in-band describe element. The schema is self-describing via its own appinfo annotations.

spec:clayers.llm:schema: The LLM layer embeds descriptions optimized for language model consumption. Two element types: <node> describes a spec node by ID reference (validated via keyref), and <schema> describes a schema or a specific schema element by namespace URI. This layer is self-describing: it contains a description of its own schema. Use it to provide context about what each element means, how to use it, and how it relates to other elements.

Describes a spec node for LLM consumption. The ref attribute is a node ID (validated against the unified ID space). The text content should explain what the node means, how it relates to other nodes, and any guidance for interpreting or modifying it.

Describes a schema or a specific element within a schema. The namespace attribute is the schema's namespace URI. The optional element attribute narrows the description to a specific element within that schema. When element is omitted, the description applies to the schema as a whole.
spec:clayers.llm:node: Describes the LLM layer's dual-mode design: in-band descriptions inside xs:appinfo in XSD files, and out-of-band descriptions in llm.xml with node (by ID) and schema (by namespace) elements. The layer is self-describing. Key for understanding how to add LLM guidance to new schemas or spec nodes.
